title: Generation Failure: Ignoring Good Context
concept: Cases where retrieval succeeded but the model still hallucinated or ignored the provided grounding.

exercises.prompt: In the previous lesson, we learned about 'Retrieval Failure'. What is the main characteristic of this type of failure?

options.text: The retriever returned irrelevant chunks because of an embedding mismatch or bad chunking.
The LLM successfully generated a perfect response using old training data.
The user asked a query that had nothing to do with the application.

exercises.explain: If the retriever returns irrelevant documents, it means the retrieval stage itself failed (due to bad embeddings, poor chunking, or missing data in the database).

cards.title: The Perfect Search, The Wrong Answer
cards.body: What happens when your search engine works perfectly, but your AI still makes things up? It's like finding the perfect recipe book but deciding to cook from memory anyway! Let's explore why LLMs ignore the facts right in front of them.

cards.title: Generation Failure Defined
cards.body: Generation Failure occurs when your retrieval system successfully finds the right context, but the LLM ignores that information, hallucinating an answer based on its pre-training data instead.

cards.title: The Open-Book Guesswork
cards.body: Imagine giving an open-book exam to a student. The student has the exact correct page open in front of them, but they decide to guess the answer using their memory anyway. The model is doing the exact same thing when it ignores retrieved context.

cards.title: A Classic Disconnect
cards.body: You search for 'Refund policy for 2024'. The retriever pulls the 2024 policy document. However, the LLM ignores it and outputs: 'Refunds are given within 30 days' because that was the policy in its 2023 training data.

cards.title: The Myth of Perfect Attention
cards.body: Many believe that if you feed the correct document to an LLM, it will always use it. In reality, LLMs are biased toward patterns they learned during training, and they can easily overlook facts in the context if they conflict with their inner weights.

cards.title: Input vs. Output Mismatch
cards.body: Compare what the LLM receives versus how it responds when generation fails.

right.text: 'The price is $99.99' (relying on old training data).

exercises.prompt: If your search engine successfully finds the exact paragraph containing the answer, but the LLM still hallucinates a wrong answer, this is classified as:

options.text: A retrieval failure, because the chunk was bad.
A generation failure, because the LLM ignored the retrieved context.
An embedding failure, because the query vector was misaligned.
A database failure, because the metadata was corrupted.

exercises.explain: When a model ignores the correct context and outputs what it remembers from its training data, it is experiencing a generation failure, not a retrieval failure.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If your retrieval system has 100% accuracy in finding relevant documents, your RAG system is guaranteed to never hallucinate.
exercises.explain: Even with a perfect retriever, prompt design and model settings are critical to make sure the LLM actually reads and respects the retrieved context.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of generation failure:
exercises.sentence: When the context contains too much extra info, it acts as a distraction that causes the LLM to ignore the correct facts.
exercises.explain: Irrelevant text acts as a distraction, drawing the LLM's attention away from the correct answers hidden in the context.

exercises.prompt: Identify the output where the AI model ignored the retrieved context:

options.text: Context: 'Our store is closed on Mondays.' Prompt: 'Is the store open on Monday?' Response: 'No, the store is closed on Mondays.'
Context: 'Our store is closed on Mondays.' Prompt: 'Is the store open on Monday?' Response: 'I cannot answer based on the provided text.'
Context: 'Our store is closed on Mondays.' Prompt: 'Is the store open on Monday?' Response: 'Yes! Most stores are open on Mondays from 9 AM to 5 PM.'

exercises.explain: Response C shows the LLM ignoring the specific constraint in the retrieved context (closed on Mondays) and hallucinating a generic answer based on its pre-existing training weights.

exercises.prompt: Your RAG system successfully retrieves the correct price sheet, but the LLM still insists on outputting an outdated price from its training data. Which of the following is the best first step to fix this generation failure?

options.text: Add more documents to the context window to overwhelm the model.
Instruct the model in the prompt to prioritize context over prior knowledge.
Increase the model's temperature to make its generation more creative.
Switch from dense embeddings to sparse BM25 keyword matching.

exercises.explain: When the context contains the correct answer but the model ignores it due to pre-training bias, you need to forcefully guide it. Modifying the system prompt to explicitly command the model to rely only on the provided context (and to say 'I don't know' if it's missing) is the most direct and effective fix.
